task_completion.description: Agent opens or writes code to read the input dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a script (e.g., Python, R) reading '~/pebl/data/snarc_data.csv', or the file being opened in a spreadsheet/data viewer.

task_completion.description: Agent identifies the contaminated participant based on invariant reaction times or accuracy.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code logic filtering for invariant RTs/accuracy, terminal output showing data exploration that flags 'sub-99', or explicit mention of 'sub-99' having 45.0ms RTs.

task_completion.description: Agent computes mean accuracy and SNARC slope for each participant.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing calculations for mean accuracy (before filtering) and within-subject regression (Lorch & Myers method) to find the SNARC slope for each participant.

task_completion.description: Agent computes the group mean SNARC slope excluding the contaminated participant.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or calculations showing the aggregation (mean) of the individual SNARC slopes for valid participants only (excluding sub-99).

task_completion.description: Agent formats and saves the final JSON report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'snarc_report.json' in '~/pebl/analysis/' containing a 'participants' list (with id, snarc_slope_ms_per_digit, mean_accuracy) and a 'group_mean_snarc_slope' field.

integrity.description: Agent performed actual calculations rather than hallucinating the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of a script (Python, R, etc.) or statistical software usage that processes the data, rather than a manually typed JSON file with guessed numbers.

integrity.description: Agent inspected the data to find the anomaly rather than using privileged knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent wrote exploratory code (e.g., checking min/max/std of RTs) or viewed the CSV to discover 'sub-99', rather than hardcoding the exclusion of 'sub-99' without prior inspection.

integrity.description: Agent saved the output to the correct file path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON file is genuinely saved to the file system at '~/pebl/analysis/snarc_report.json' (visible via text editor save path, file explorer, or terminal 'ls'/'cat' commands), not just printed to standard output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 15 valid participants (sub-01 to sub-15) and one contaminated participant (sub-99). The contaminated participant, sub-99, has impossibly fast and invariant reaction times of exactly 45.0ms with 100% accuracy across all trials. The required analytical method is Lorch & Myers (1990) within-subject regression. The final output must be saved to /home/ga/pebl/analysis/snarc_report.json.
